mnemonics: NLP Needs Careful Processing - Never Let Preprocessing Slide
keyPoints: Always preprocess text data (tokenization, stopword removal, stemming/lemmatization) before feeding it to an NLP model.
Understand the fundamental difference between stemming (crude chop) and lemmatization (contextual, dictionary-based reduction).
The choice of learning resources (research papers, official docs, tutorials) depends heavily on your current learning goal (theory vs. implementation).
commonMistakes: Skipping text preprocessing, leading to poor model performance and garbage-in-garbage-out results.
Using stemming when lemmatization is more appropriate for the task, losing crucial word meaning.
Selecting advanced research papers as a starting point instead of foundational tutorials, leading to confusion and frustration.
